title: Charles Cain
article_plain_text: Charles 'Chip' Cain Status Deceased Profession Manager of the Dent's Shooting Club Affiliation Alphabet Crimes ( formerly ) Maxwell Dent ( formerly ) Portrayed By Dean Stockwell " Goodbye, Mr. Cain " ―Karla Fry's last words to Chip Cain. Charles 'Chip' Cain is the manager of Maxwell Dent 's Beverly Hills Shooting Club and the tertiary antagonist in Beverly Hills Cop II . Biography [ ] Beverly Hills Cop II [ ] Posing as an undercover FBI agent, Axel Foley finds out that the ammunition fired at one of the robberies was designed by the manager of a gun club owned by Maxwell Dent. Foley has Bogomil's daughter Jan use her connections as an insurance agent to find out about Dent's financial dealings. Dent is robbing his own businesses on purpose in order to finance firearms transactions with an arms dealer named Nikos Thomopolis and is discreetly using Cain as the front man for his operations. Bogomil was shot because his investigation was on the correct track into the case. Having foiled a robbery attempt at a bank depot, Foley is able to trick Dent's accountant Sidney Bernstein into using his computer and discovers that Dent and Karla are planning to leave the country. Foley also learns from Jan that all of Dent's businesses have had their insurance coverage canceled and are about to go bankrupt except his race track, which he is convinced is the next target. On the way to the race track, Foley solves the latest riddle sent to the police, and is convinced that this riddle was made easily solvable in order to implicate Cain as the Alphabet Bandit. However, Foley knows Cain is a patsy designed to throw the authorities off Dent's trail. During the robbery, Cain is shot in the head by Karla with her Desert Eagle, making it look like Cain was killed in his own botched heist. This led Harold Lutz to falsely claim that the crimes had been solved. Eventually following the final battle, Foley, Taggert, and Rosewood were able to prove on Dent being the real Alphabet Bandit. title: Jane Saunders
article_plain_text: Jane Saunders Born March 24, 1991 Status Alive Aliases Jane Foley (Birthname) Janey (Alias by Rosewood and Taggart) Dorothy Hrbek (Alias) Jacqueline (Alias) Profession Defense Attorney Residence 1441 North Doheny Drive Apartment 107 Beverly Hills , California [ 1 ] [ ] Detroit , Michigan (Formerly) Affiliation Detroit Police Department (Via Axel) Beverly Hills Police Department McGregor & Peterman (Attorney Office) Family Members Axel Foley (Father) Unnamed Mother Portrayed By Taylour Paige Jane Nova Saunders is a character in Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F , portrayed by Taylour Paige . She is the daughter of Axel Foley and a lawyer in Beverly Hills . Contents 1 Biography 1 Biography 1.1 Early life 1.1 Early life 1.2 Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F 1.2 Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F 2 Personality 2 Personality 3 Trivia 3 Trivia 4 Gallery 4 Gallery 5 References 5 References Biography [ ] Early life [ ] Jane was born on March 24, 1991, in Detroit, Michigan, to Axel Foley and his wife. She grew up learning how to escape handcuffs, escape the trunk of a car, defend herself, and other police tactics. When she was older, the 12th Street Mafia vowed to execute Axel's family, so he moved them to California. As the family stayed apart, Mrs. Foley told Axel to stay in Detroit and get a divorce. This caused a fallout between Axel and Jane, whose contact waned over the years. She legally changed her surname from Foley to Saunders. Jane became a lawyer and was hired by a private criminal defense office in Beverly Hills . She met Detective Bobby Abbott and they had a relationship, though she ghosted him after a while. Her and Axel and didn't speak for over five years. Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F [ ] Billy Rosewood thinks Sam Enriquez is being framed by Cade Grant and other dirty cops for murder, so he asks Jane if anyone in her first can take Enriquez's case. She volunteers to take it after reading the file. When her life is threatened as a result of the case, her estranged father Axel Foley visits Beverly Hills to help her. They form a rocky alliance to prove her clients innocence, save a missing Rosewood, and stop a crew of dirty cops. Personality [ ] Jane is Axel’s estranged daughter. She is a Beverly Hills-based defense attorney. Trivia [ ] In the CBS pilot that was filmed, Axel had a son named Aaron who was played by Brandon T. Jackson. [ 2 ] [ ] The character was retconned for the film continuation and became Jane Saunders. Jane is bilingual, speaking fluent English and Spanish. According to Axel, she is a fan of Mary J. Blige. title: Joseph Gordon-Levitt
article_plain_text: Joseph Gordon-Levitt Born Joseph Leonard Gordon-Levitt February 17, 1981 (age 43) Los Angeles, California Years Active 1988-present Occupation Actor Character Bobby Abbott Film(s) Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F Joseph Gordon-Levitt an an American actor. He stars in Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F as Detective Bobby Abbott . Overview [ ] Joseph Gordon-Levitt is a highly accoladed actor, receiving over a dozen nominations for his work over the years. He began acting in 1988 with appearances in TV films and series. He reached public notoriety after his performance in Angels in the Outfield (1994), 3rd Rock from the Sun (1996-2001), Halloween H20: 20 Years Later and 10 Things I Hate About You . More work included 500 Days of Summer (2009), G.I. Joe: The Rise of Cobra (2009), Inception (2010), and dozens more. Gordon-Levitt is also an accomplished musician, having published two compilation albums, one soundtrack album, and sixteen singles. He is also a credited director, writer, and producer on several projects. Notes and Trivia [ ] Joseph has shared the screen with his Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F co-star Judge Reinhold before in the 1992 film Beethoven , though they didn't share any scenes together. This was Joseph's film debut. title: Bobby Abbott
article_plain_text: Bobby Abbott Status Alive Aliases Chad (Alias) Profession Police Detective Residence Beverly Hills , California Affiliation Detroit Police Department LAPD Air Support (Formerly) Portrayed By Joseph Gordon-Levitt Bobby Abbott is a character in Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F , portrayed by Joseph Gordon-Levitt . Bobby is a police Detective with the Beverly Hills Police Department . Contents 1 Biography 1 Biography 1.1 Early life 1.1 Early life 1.2 Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F 1.2 Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F 2 Personality 2 Personality 3 Trivia 3 Trivia 4 Gallery 4 Gallery 5 References 5 References Biography [ ] Early life [ ] Bobby was born in Chicago, though his father moved his family to Beverly Hills . A year later, Bobby's father left him and his mother and they were estranged for decades. Bobby became a helicopter pilot in the Los Angeles Police Department: Air Support Division. After crashing a helicopter he resigned from the LAPD and became a Detective with the Beverly Hills Police Department (BHPD). Beverly Hills Cop: Axel F [ ] Personality [ ] Detective Bobby Abbott is a diligent and committed detective. He is determined and focused on cracking the murder conspiracy case at the heart of the film. Despite his personal history with Jane Saunders , he maintains a professional demeanor and is dedicated to uncovering the truth. His ability to collaborate effectively with Axel Foley suggests he is adaptable and able to work well under pressure, displaying both competence and reliability in his role as a detective.
